title: 第 23 節
en: Mohammed shall not forget any of the revelation save what God pleases. ‘The revelation is the same as that given to Abraham and Moses.
LXXXVIII. Tue Carrer oF THE OvERWHELMING. (Mecca.)
Description of the last day, heaven, and hell.
LXXXIX. Tue Cuapter or THE Dawn. (Mecca.)
Fate of previous nations who rejected the apostles. Admonition to those who rely too much on their prosperity.
XC. Tue CuapTer or THE Lanp. (Mecca.) Exhortation to practise charity.
XCI. THe Cuapter oF THE Sun. (Mecca.) Purity of the soul brings happiness. Example of Thamfd.
XCIl. Tue Cuaprer or THE Nicut. (Mecca.) Promise of reward to believers and of punishment to idolaters.

XCIII. THe Cuapter or THE Forenoon. (Mecca.) Mohammed encouraged and bidden to remember how God has OF THE QUR'AN. CXVli cared for him hitherto; he is to be charitable in return, and to publish God’s goodness.
XCIV. Tue Cuapter or ‘HAvE WE NOT EXPANDED?’ (Mecca.) God has made Mohammed’s mission easier to him.
XCV. Tue CuHapTer oF THE Fic. (Place of origin doubtful.) The degradation of man: future reward and punishment.

XCVI. Tue CuapTer or ConceaLeD Broop. (Mecca.) Mohammed’s first call to ‘Read’ the Qur'an. Denunciation of Abu Laheb for his opposition. XCVII. Tue Cuaprer or ‘Power.’ (Place of origin doubtful.) The Qur'an revealed on ‘the night of power.’ Its excellence: angels descend thereon. XCVIII. Tue Cuaprer or THE Manirest Sien. (Place of origin doubtful.) Rebuke to Jews and Christians for doubting the manifest sign of Mohammed’s mission. XCIX. Tue CHaprer oF THE Eartuquake. (Place of origin doubtful.) The earthquake preceding the judgment day.
C, THe CuHapTer oF THE Cuarcers. (Mecca.)
Oath by the charging of war horses. Man is ungrateful: certainty of the judgment.
CI. Tue Cyaprer oF THE Smitinc. (Mecca.) The terrors of the last day and of hell-fire.
CII. Tue CHAPTER oF THE CONTENTION ABOUT NUMBERS. (Place of origin doubtful.)
Two families of the Arabs rebuked for contending which was the more numerous. Warning of the punishment of hell.
CIII. Tue Cuapter or THE Arternoon. (Mecca.) Believers only shall prosper.
CIV. Tue CwaptTer or THE BacxpiTer. (Mecca.) Backbiters shall be cast into hell.
CXViii ABSTRACT OF THE CONTENTS OF THE QURAN.
CV. Tue Cuaprer or THE Exvepuant. (Mecca.)
The miraculous destruction of the Abyssinian army under Abraha al Asram by birds when invading Mecca with elephants.
CVI. Tur CHAPTER OF THE QurAis. (Mecca.)
The Qurdis are bidden to give thanks to God for the trade of their two yearly caravans.
CVII. Tue Cuapter or ‘Necessarizs.’ (Place of origin doubtful.) Denunciation of the unbelieving and uncharitable.
CVIII. Tur Cuapter or Et KAutuar. (Mecca.)
Mohammed is commanded to offer the sacrifices out of his abundance. Threat that his enemies shall be childless.
CIX. Tue CuapTer or THE Mispeuievers. (Mecca.) The prophet will not follow the religion of the misbelievers.
CX. Tue Carter or Herp. (Mecca.) Prophecy that men shall join Islim by troops.
CXI. Tuer CuapTer or Apu Lanes. (Mecca.)
